CheckTitle: Ensure repository rulesets dismiss stale reviews when new commits are pushed
Description: Ensures that at least one active GitHub Repository Ruleset is configured to dismiss stale pull request approvals when new commits are pushed. Without this, a previously approved PR could be merged even after new code is added without re-review.
Risk: If stale reviews are not dismissed on push, a malicious actor could get a PR approved and then push additional changes without requiring a fresh review, potentially introducing unauthorized code.

Recommendation.Text: Go to repository Settings > Rules > Rulesets. Create or edit a ruleset targeting your default branch. Under 'Require a pull request before merging', enable 'Dismiss stale pull request approvals when new commits are pushed'.

Notes: This check prioritises GitHub Repository Rulesets over legacy branch protection rules. Repositories still using legacy rules receive MANUAL status with migration guidance. Archived repositories are skipped.
